assertions.text: V1 was traveling westbound in lane two of a five-lane undivided roadway (two westbound lanes, two eastbound lanes, and a center turn lane).
V1 traversed the lane lines and entered lane one (the adjacent westbound lane) before departing the left side of the roadway.
V1 departed the left side of the roadway and initiated a clockwise yaw (rotation).
V1's left side impacted an overhead traffic signal pole.
The driver of V1 was ejected from the vehicle during the collision sequence.
After impacting the pole, the vehicle rotated rapidly clockwise around the pole and split into two pieces.
The vehicle struck the overhead traffic signal pole and then the front portion impacted a concrete electric box — at least two distinct impacts occurred.
The vehicle split into two pieces: a rear box and a front section.
The rear box (rear portion) came to rest in the center of the roadway.
The front portion continued westerly after separation and impacted a concrete electric box with its right side plane.
After impacting the concrete electric box, the front portion came to rest facing easterly.
The vehicle split into two pieces (indicating a severe collision).

verdicts.evidence: The claim states lane positioning and roadway configuration which are not present in the telemetry channels. The available data shows vehicle speed values, e.g. [first_value 156.2](T2) km/h and [last_value 122.4](T2) km/h, but no lane or map/GPS channels to confirm direction or lane count.

Lane crossing and which lane entered are not recorded by the available channels. Steering shows a large deviation of [peak_abs_dev 91.5 deg at t=-0.9 s](T2) but that alone cannot confirm lane line traversal or entering lane one.

Telemetry records rotation-related signals only indirectly; steering shows a large signed peak of [-91.5 deg at t=-0.9 s](T2) and crash-pulse lateral delta-V reached [4.3 km/h at t=0.21 s](T1), but device-frame yaw direction and whether it is clockwise cannot be determined without sensor-to-vehicle-frame alignment, so direction of rotation is unverifiable.

The assertion about the left side impacting an overhead traffic signal pole is about impact location and object type; sensors record cumulative delta-V (e.g. [delta_v_long final_value 7.3 km/h](T2) and [delta_v_lat final_value 4.1 km/h](T2)) and impact magnitudes but do not identify which vehicle side or object struck, so this is unverifiable.

Ejection of the driver is a occupant outcome not captured in the vehicle telemetry. The EDR summary shows driver belt code [1](T1) (buckled) but no explicit ejection flag, so ejection is unverifiable from the sensors.

Telemetry shows a crash pulse with cumulative deltas ([longitudinal max ~7.3 km/h at t=0.24 s](T2) and [lateral max ~4.3 km/h at t=0.21 s](T2)), and detect_events reports multiple impact detections ([n_detections 7](T3)), but specifics about rotating around the pole and splitting into pieces are physical/visual sequence details not present in the telemetry, so unverifiable.

The telemetry records multiple crash-related impacts: detect_events reports two crash-pulse delta-V maxima, with [delta_v_long magnitude_kmh 7.7 at t=0.251 s](T3) and [delta_v_lat magnitude_kmh 4.3 at t=0.21 s](T3); additionally the detector count shows [num_detections 7](T3) and EDR summary lists [num_events_recorded 3](T1), supporting that at least two distinct impact impulses occurred.

The claim that the vehicle split into two named pieces (rear box and front section) is a visual/structural observation not present in telemetry. Sensors show crash magnitude ([max_delta_v_long_kmh 10.85](T1)) but cannot confirm separation into two identifiable pieces.

Resting position of the rear portion in the roadway center is a location observation not in the telemetry. The device records speeds and delta-Vs ([speed first 156.2 km/h](T2)) but no post-crash GPS positions, so unverifiable.

The claim about the front portion continuing westerly and impacting a concrete electric box on its right side is not detectable in the available channels. While there is a lateral delta-V of [4.3 km/h](T2), the object type and which vehicle side impacted are not recorded, so unverifiable.

Final orientation (facing easterly) of the front portion after impact is a pose/location detail not available in telemetry; sensors provide delta-V magnitudes ([delta_v_long final_value 7.3 km/h](T2)) but not final heading, so unverifiable.

Severity claim (split into two pieces indicating severe collision) is supported by crash pulse magnitudes: EDR summary reports [max_delta_v_long_kmh 10.85](T1) and detect_events shows a delta-V event of [7.7 km/h at t=0.251 s](T3); such delta-V magnitudes and multiple impact detections ([num_detections 7](T3)) are consistent with a severe collision likely to cause major structural damage.

result.rationale: Telemetry provides clear crash-pulse magnitudes and multiple impact detections (e.g. [max_delta_v_long_kmh 10.85](T1); [delta_v_lat magnitude_kmh 4.3](T3); [n_detections 7](T3)), supporting that a significant multi-impact crash occurred, but lane-level, object-type, occupant-ejection, rotational direction, and final rest-position assertions are not recorded and therefore unverifiable.

citations.claim: longitudinal max ~7.3 km/h at t=0.24 s
lateral max ~4.3 km/h at t=0.21 s

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
